@@ -78,7 +78,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-308","title":"Auto-init on first run","description":"**Automatically initialize config on first sync**

## What
When user runs `ru sync` with no config, automatically initialize instead of erroring.

## Why
Forcing `ru init` as a separate step is poor UX. Modern CLI tools should 'just work' on first run.

## Behavior
```bash
$ ru sync
First run detected. Initializing configuration...
  Created ~/.config/ru/config
  Created ~/.config/ru/repos.d/repos.txt

No repositories configured yet.
Add repos with: ru add owner/repo

$ echo 'But wait, what if they pass a URL directly?'

$ ru sync https://github.com/owner/repo
First run detected. Initializing configuration...
  Created ~/.config/ru/config  
  Created ~/.config/ru/repos.d/repos.txt

Adding and syncing: owner/repo
  Cloned: owner/repo
```

## Implementation
In cmd_sync(), before processing:
```bash
if [[ ! -d "$RU_CONFIG_DIR" ]]; then
    log_info "First run detected. Initializing configuration..."
    ensure_config_exists
    # If args provided, treat as ad-hoc sync
    # If no args, explain how to add repos
fi
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- First sync auto-initializes
- If URL/repo passed, add and sync it
- If no args, explain next steps
- `ru init` still works for explicit initialization","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:10:05.881422408-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-03T16:28:23.339456108-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-03T16:28:23.339456108-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented auto-init in cmd_sync() at lines 1129-1185. On first run, automatically initializes config. Supports ad-hoc repo syncing via positional args.","labels":["config","ux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-308","depends_on_id":"bd-3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:13.619210776-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"bd-308","depends_on_id":"bd-305","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:13.648556659-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"bd-309","title":"Simplify to single repos.txt by default","description":"**Use single repos.txt file instead of public/private split**

## What
Change from public.txt + private.txt to single repos.txt file.

## Why
The public/private split adds complexity without clear benefit:
- gh handles auth automatically
- Most users don't need the separation
- Simpler mental model = better UX

## Design
```
~/.config/ru/repos.d/
└── repos.txt          # All repos, public and private
```

If users want organization, they can add comments:
```
# My projects
owner/repo1
owner/repo2

# Work projects (private)
company/internal-tool
company/secret-project
```

## Migration
If old public.txt/private.txt exist:
1. Merge into repos.txt
2. Keep originals as backup
3. Log migration message

## ru add Changes
- Remove --private flag (no longer needed)
- Just add to repos.txt

## Acceptance Criteria
- Single repos.txt is default
- Old format auto-migrates
- `ru add` simplified
- Comments work for organization","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:10:05.881422408-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-03T16:29:00.352604542-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-03T16:29:00.352604542-05:00","close_reason":"Already implemented - using single repos.txt by default (line 497)","labels":["config","simplicity"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-309","depends_on_id":"bd-3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:13.678924519-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"bd-309","depends_on_id":"bd-305","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:13.70835933-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"bd-310","title":"Change default PROJECTS_DIR to ~/projects","description":"**Use ~/projects as default instead of /data/projects**

## What
Change DEFAULT_PROJECTS_DIR from /data/projects to ~/projects.

## Why
/data/projects is very specific and unusual:
- Most users don't have /data
- It's not a standard path
- ~/projects is more universally appropriate

## Implementation
```bash
# Old
DEFAULT_PROJECTS_DIR="/data/projects"

# New
DEFAULT_PROJECTS_DIR="$HOME/projects"
```

## First Run
On first run, if ~/projects doesn't exist, ask:
```
Where should repositories be cloned?
  [1] ~/projects (will be created)
  [2] Current directory: /path/to/cwd
  [3] Custom path
```

Or just default to ~/projects and create it.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Default is ~/projects
- Works on macOS and Linux
- Creates directory if needed
- User can override via config","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:10:05.881422408-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-03T16:28:59.31411541-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-03T16:28:59.31411541-05:00","close_reason":"Already implemented - DEFAULT_PROJECTS_DIR is set to $HOME/projects at line 88","labels":["config","ux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-310","depends_on_id":"bd-3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:13.736775722-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"bd-310","depends_on_id":"bd-203","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:13.767564444-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"bd-905","title":"Implement get_all_repos()","description":"**Load and merge all repository lists**

## What
Function to load all list files, merge, dedupe, and check for collisions.

## Why
This is the main entry point for getting the list of repos to process.

## Implementation
```bash
get_all_repos() {
    local repos_dir="$RU_CONFIG_DIR/repos.d"
    
    if [[ ! -d "$repos_dir" ]]; then
        log_warn "No repos.d directory. Run 'ru init' first."
        return 0
    fi
    
    # Collect all repos from all .txt files
    local all_repos
    all_repos=$(
        for f in "$repos_dir"/*.txt; do
            [[ -f "$f" ]] || continue
            load_repo_list "$f"
        done
    )
    
    if [[ -z "$all_repos" ]]; then
        log_warn "No repositories configured. Run 'ru add <repo>' first."
        return 0
    fi
    
    # Check for collisions (warning only)
    echo "$all_repos" | detect_collisions || true
    
    # Dedupe and output
    echo "$all_repos" | dedupe_repos
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- Loads from all .txt files in repos.d
- Warns about collisions
- Dedupes results
- Handles empty gracefully","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:10:05.881422408-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-03T16:33:03.267327372-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-03T16:33:03.267327372-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented all repo list management functions: parse_repo_spec (namerefs), load_repo_list, dedupe_repos, detect_collisions, get_all_repos. All tests pass.","labels":["lists"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-905","depends_on_id":"bd-901","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:11.451307773-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"bd-905","depends_on_id":"bd-903","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:11.482195622-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"bd-905","depends_on_id":"bd-904","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:11.513296182-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"bd-905b","title":"Define collision handling policy","description":"**Clear policy for path collision handling**

## What
Define what happens when collision is detected (different repos -> same path).

## Why
Current plan detects collisions but doesn't specify the behavior.

## Policy Options
1. **Warn and skip second repo** (safest, current implicit behavior)
2. **Fail fast** (strict, stops processing)
3. **Prompt user** (interactive only)
4. **Auto-rename with suffix** (risky)

## Chosen Policy: Warn and skip
```
Collision detected:
  Path: /home/user/projects/utils
  Configured: org1/utils (will be synced)
  Skipped:    org2/utils (same path)

To fix: Change layout to 'owner-repo' in config:
  ru config --set LAYOUT=owner-repo
```

## Implementation
- First occurrence wins
- Log warning with resolution hint
- Include in summary as 'skipped:collision'
- Exit code 2 (conflicts) if any collisions

## Acceptance Criteria
- Clear messaging about collision
- First-wins policy
- Resolution hint in output
- Documented in README","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:10:05.881422408-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-03T16:30:11.250738889-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-03T16:30:11.250738889-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented in ru script SECTION 9B","labels":["lists","safety"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-905b","depends_on_id":"bd-9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:13.858988726-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"bd-905b","depends_on_id":"bd-904","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:13.890572977-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
